lesson.title: Matrix Operations
lesson.body: Matrices support addition (component-wise, same dimensions), scalar multiplication, and matrix multiplication (AB where columns of B must equal rows of A). Matrix multiplication is associative but generally NOT commutative: AB ≠ BA. The transpose Aᵀ is obtained by reflecting across the main diagonal. The identity matrix I satisfies AI = IA = A for any compatible matrix A.

rooms.question: Compute the (1,1) entry of AB where A = [[1,2],[3,4]] and B = [[1,0],[0,1]].
rooms.explanation: B is the identity matrix, so AB = A. The (1,1) entry of A is 1.

rooms.question: For which pair of matrices is it guaranteed that AB = BA?
steps: Matrix multiplication is generally non-commutative. Exceptions: I, scalar multiples of I, or A and its own powers.
answers: A = I (identity) and B = any square matrix
A and B are any two 2×2 matrices
A and B are both diagonal matrices with distinct entries
A and B are square matrices of different sizes
rooms.explanation: The identity matrix I commutes with every square matrix of the same size: IA = AI = A. In general AB ≠ BA.

rooms.question: If A is a 3×4 matrix and B is a 4×2 matrix, what is the size of AB?
steps: The product A_{m\times k} \cdot B_{k\times n} has dimensions m \times n.
rooms.explanation: A is 3×4 and B is 4×2. The product AB is 3×2 (inner dimensions 4 must match; outer dimensions give result size).

rooms.question: Order the steps to compute AB for A = [[2,1],[0,3]] and B = [[1,4],[2,-1]]:
items: Identify dimensions: A is 2×2, B is 2×2, so AB is 2×2
rooms.explanation: First verify compatibility, then compute each row by taking dot products of A's rows with B's columns.

boss.name: The Non-Commutative
boss.taunt: Reverse the order and watch your entire solution unravel!

questions.question: If A is an m×n matrix, what is the size of Aᵀ?
questions.explanation: The transpose Aᵀ is obtained by swapping rows and columns, so an m×n matrix becomes n×m.

questions.question: Given A = [[1,2],[3,4]], what is (Aᵀ)ᵀ?
questions.explanation: Transposing twice returns the original matrix: (Aᵀ)ᵀ = A.

questions.question: Which property of matrix multiplication is correct?
answers: A(B+C) = AB + AC fails in general
questions.explanation: Matrix multiplication is associative: A(BC) = (AB)C always holds. It is NOT commutative in general, but IS distributive over addition.
